messages.content: You are CYB(ri)AN, an expert AI Cybersecurity Analyst developed to assist IT and security teams. You provide accurate information based on internal documentation, security policies, and technical guidelines.
No relevant information found in the internal documentation.

I couldn’t find your onboarding protocol in the handbook (ran the internal doc search and got no results). I also don’t have a separate “password tool” integration available here, but I evaluated NovaCore2026! using standard password-strength heuristics (length, character classes, and common-pattern risks). Here are the results and a clear yes/no rule you can apply once you tell me the protocol minimum.

Quick facts about NovaCore2026!
- Length: 13 characters.
- Character classes present: uppercase (N, C), lowercase (o,v,a,o,r,e), digits (2,0,2,6), symbol (!). So it uses 4 character classes.
- Composition: two dictionary-like word segments (“Nova” and “Core”) + a 4‑digit year + a trailing symbol. Those predictable elements reduce effective entropy compared with a truly random string.

Strength estimate
- Theoretical entropy if fully random from 94 printable ASCII ≈ 85 bits (13 × ~6.55 bits/char). That is an upper bound and not realistic here.
- Practical entropy estimate given common-word + year pattern: roughly in the 25–40 bit range (depends on attacker model). In other words, it’s significantly weaker than a 13-character random password because it contains recognizable words and a year.

Does it meet the onboarding-protocol minimum?
- I could not find your onboarding protocol so I don’t know the exact minimum. If your protocol’s minimum permanent-passphrase length is:
  - ≤ 13 characters: Yes, NovaCore2026! meets the minimum length.
  - > 13 characters (for example, 16, 20, or a passphrase requirement like 3–4 random words): No, it would not meet that minimum.
Please paste the onboarding-protocol line (or tell me the required minimum length) and I’ll confirm definitively.

Recommendations
- If this will be used as a permanent passphrase, strengthen it. Options:
  - Use a true passphrase of multiple random words (Diceware-style) totaling ≥ 16–20 characters.
  - Or increase uniqueness: avoid dictionary words and predictable years; add length (≥ 16) and retain variety of character classes.
  - Store and generate long, unique passphrases with a corporate password manager; enable MFA.
- If you must keep this style, at minimum increase length (e.g., NovaCore!x7B2q#2026 → but better: long random passphrase).

Tell me the exact minimum required by the onboarding protocol (or paste that section) and I’ll state definitively whether NovaCore2026! complies and give a suggested replacement that would.
I apologize, but I failed to receive a response from the model.
Please use your analyze_phishing_email tool to evaluate this exact text: 'Subject: Routine System Update. Body: The maintenance window is scheduled for midnight. Sender: internal-ops@novacore.com'.
I apologize, but I failed to receive a response from the model.
